borrower_1_occupation_if_unemployed_give.answer: Senior Network Engineer at a mid-sized IT services firm, responsible for maintaining corporate and client network infrastructure, supervising a small team of junior engineers, and managing on-call support rotations.

borrower_2_occupation_if_unemployed_give.answer: Primary school teacher (3rd class) at a local public school, responsible for classroom instruction, lesson planning, parent-teacher communication, and extracurricular literacy activities.

borrower_1_name_of_employer_length_of_se.answer: DigitalConnect Solutions Ltd., Park West Business Park, Dublin 12
Length of service: 6 years full-time (with the company since May 2019)

borrower_2_name_of_employer_length_of_se.answer: St. Brigid's National School, Castleknock, Dublin 15
Length of service: 8 years full-time (with the school since September 2017)

reasons_for_review_arrears_line_1.answer: We are requesting a review and assistance with mortgage arrears that began to accumulate over the last 8 months due to a combination of reduced household income and increased essential living costs. In August of last year, Michael's employer implemented a company-wide cost-cutting programme, which resulted in his overtime hours being significantly reduced and his take-home pay dropping by approximately 15–20% per month. For several years, a substantial portion of our regular mortgage repayment was covered by predictable overtime and on-call allowances, so the sudden reduction in hours had a direct and immediate impact on our ability to meet the full monthly repayment on time.

reasons_for_review_arrears_line_2.answer: At the same time, Emma had to temporarily reduce her working hours from full-time to 3.5 days per week from September through December in order to care for our younger child, who has been undergoing assessment and follow-up appointments for a suspected learning difficulty and mild asthma. This reduction in hours meant a further drop of about 25% in her net income during that period. In addition, we experienced several unexpected but unavoidable expenses, including: (1) approximately €1,200 in medical and specialist consultation costs not fully covered by insurance; (2) a major car repair in November (clutch and timing belt replacement) totalling around €1,050; and (3) increased childcare costs on the days Emma was at the hospital or at specialist appointments, where we had to rely on a registered childminder at short notice.

reasons_for_review_arrears_line_3.answer: Because these events happened within a relatively short timeframe, we fell behind on two full mortgage payments and have since been paying what we can afford each month, which has been slightly below the required amount. We have not ignored the situation; instead, we have prioritised keeping up with essential utilities, food, school expenses, and transportation while making partial payments toward the mortgage whenever possible. Emma has now returned to full-time hours as of January, and Michael's overtime has partially resumed, so our income has stabilised, but we are still struggling to clear the accumulated arrears in one lump sum. We are therefore seeking a structured arrangement—such as a temporary reduction in monthly payments, an extension of the mortgage term, or a formal arrears repayment plan added on top of our normal payment—that will allow us to clear the arrears in a realistic timeframe without falling behind again, while maintaining a stable home for our two young children.
